RUTAN & TUCKER
ATTORNEYS AT LAW

A PARTNERSHIP INCLUDING PROFESSIONAL CORPORATIONS

GARVIN F. SMALLENBERGER    STAN WOLCOTT*           CENTRAL BANK TOWER, SUITE 300      DAVID M. HOCHNER
ALFRED J. RUTAN*            ROBERT N. TUCKER       SOUTH COAST PLAZA TOWER            SANFORD SWATZ
TUCKER R. ETTER             WILLIAM H. FERGUSON    611 ANTON BOULEVARD                STEPHEN B. OTTO
JAMES W. RUTAN*             WILLIAM M. MARTICORENA                                    BARBARA A. NISHKIAN
RICHARD C. GUNNIFF          LAURENCE BOYD          POST OFFICE BOX 950                LARRY E. PARKER
LEONARD A. HAYNES           ROBERT L. CARROLL                                         JAMES P. FLAHERTY
ALFRED C. RUTAN*            DONALD J. LYNCH        COSTA MESA, CALIFORNIA 92626-9950  KENT HARRISON
MICHAEL W. IMHELL           JAMES E. JORGENSEN                                        JAMES A. FERGUSON
THOMAS M. WALSH             RICHARD K. CARSON      TELEPHONE (714) 641-5100           SCOTT M. SCHOENEWALD
ROBERT W. WALLACE, JR.*      JAMES R. CURRY        (213) 625-7586                     CRAIG S. SLOAN
WILLIAM LAUGHLIN            FREDERICK GOLTZER                                         KENT B. MCBRIDE
RICHARD P. SIME             JOEL D. KUPERBERG      TELECOPIER (714) 546-9035          DAN SLATEN
BENJAMIN C. PEACEMAN        JAMES K. JOHNSON                                          PAULA J. ROBERTS
WILLIAM S. TODD             JAMES F. JOHNSON       J.R. RUTAN (1903-1972)             STEVEN L. WOODS
EDWARD O. SVESKA, JR.*      EVRIPIDIS [VICKI] DALLS JAMES R. RUTAN, SR. (1884-1961)  DANIEL S. MAYATINE
CHARLES E. BLAUNER*         RANDALL M. MATASICH    WILSON E. RUTAN (1904-1963)        DOUGLAS B. COOPER
MICHAEL B. MORAN            PHILIP M. PRINCE       M. RODGER HOWELL (1925-1983)       DAVID P. WARD
DAVID C. LARSEN*            JOHN P. HALLISEY                                          DOUGLAS D. WHITMORE
STEPHEN R. DOOLEY           JOAN A. HALLOWAY       *A PROFESSIONAL CORPORATION        CAROL L. SEMLER
JEFFREY M. DOERMANN*        KARIN T. KROSIUS                                          RICHARD J. RUSSO
JOSEPH D. CARRUTH                                                                     JEREMY H. FRIEDMAN
                                                                                       RICHARD K. HOWELL
                                                                                       A. PATRICK MUNOZ

                                                                                       DAVID J. GARIBALDI, III
                                                                                       OF COUNSEL


                                        February 4, 1991


Fair Political Practices Commission
428 "J" Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA  95814

Attention:  Legal Division

Dear Legal Division:

     This firm serves as the City Attorney for the City of
West Covina.  This letter requests your advice on behalf of
Steve Herfert, West Covina City Councilmember.

     Councilmember Herfert is an employee of Southern
California Edison ("Edison") earning over $250 a year.  He
also participates in Edison's profit sharing plan from which
he receives over $1,000 per year.  Edison is a regulated
public utility listed on the New York Stock Exchange and a
Fortune 500 Company as described in 2 Cal. Code of Regs.
187022(a).

     The 583-acre BKK landfill is located totally within West
Covina.  It is owned and operated by the BKK Corporation.
From 1972 through November 30, 1984, the landfill accepted
hazardous materials as a Class I disposal site.  The
landfill's acceptance of such materials and the subsequent
closure of the Class I disposal area have been and continue
to be regulated by the State Department of Health Services,
other State agencies and Federal Environmental Protection
Agency and not by the city.  The City has no authority over
the Class I area.

Fair Political Practices Commission
February 4, 1991
Page 2


       However, BKK also operates a Class III (household solid
waste) disposal area.  That use continues pursuant to a city
conditional use permit ("CUP") as well as state regulation.
The City serves as the Local Enforcement Agent ("LEA") for
the Class III operation, subject to the approval of the
California Integrated Waste Management Board.  The LEA may
grant or amend a Class III landfill permit only after the
Board's concurrence.  The City Council may sit as a hearing
panel to suspend or revoke a permit but also may appoint an
independent panel.  See Public Resources Code § 44001 et.
seq. and § 44500 et seq.  Under the CUP the City may set the
maximum Class III landfill rates.  The City also may consider
entering into other agreements with BKK as necessary not
related to disposal operations.

       The City and BKK have agreed that the Class III area
will close by November 1995; that closure date is not
required by state law and could be changed by the City
Council (within the limits imposed by capacity and state
agencies).  We do not know the financial relationship within
the BKK Corporation between the two operations, although BKK
operates and is responsible for both.  We assume, but do not
know, whether any portion of the revenues from the Class III
operation are used to finance closure costs; we have been
told such costs are financed from electricity produced by the
gas turbines which dispose of methane on the site.   The
electricity is sold to Edison at rates and in an amount BKK
has not disclosed.

       As a state requirement of closure of the Class I area,
BKK was required to finance the clean-up and closure of the
site.  As such a generator, Edison (along with six or seven
others) posted a letter of credit, as described in the letter
from BKK's General Counsel, a copy of which is attached.

       The question which now has arisen is whether
Councilmember Herfert has a potential conflict of interest on
matters concerning BKK because of Edison's position as a

RUTAN & TUCKER
ATTORNEYS AT LAW
A PARTNERSHIP INCLUDING PROFESSIONAL CORPORATIONS




Fair Political Practices Commission
February 4, 1991
Page 3


guarantor of BKK's obligation.  Please let me know if you
need  additional  information  in  order  to  provide  this
analysis.  My direct line telephone number is (714) 662-4644.

                                        Very truly yours,

                                        RUTAN & TUCKER

                                        [signature]

                                        Elizabeth L. Hanna
                                        City Attorney
                                        City of West Covina

ELH:ntb
Enclosure
cc:  Steve Herfert

8/482/014125-0003/075

BKK
CORPORATION

2550 237th Street
Torrance, CA 90505
(213) 533-7150
Fax (213) 539-3933

December 17, 1990


Elizabeth L. Hanna, Esq.
RUTAN & TUCKER
611 Anton Blvd., Suite 1400
Costa Mesa, CA 92628-9990

Re: BKK Landfill

Dear Betsy:

     In response to your inquiry on Southern California Edison's (Edison)
participation in an agreement guaranteeing certain BKK Corporation financial
obligations, I can confirm that Edison is a party to an October 15, 1987
agreement known as the Participation Agreement.

     As you explained, the reason for your inquiry is to assist you in
determining whether an elected City Councilman that is currently employed
by Edison would have a conflict of interest in voting on any City matters
affecting the BKK Landfill.

     I am not at liberty to release a copy of the entire Participation
Agreement, but can disclose the following information:

1.    To facilitate the issuance of a California Pollution Control Financing
     Authority bond in the amount of approximately $12.5 million to be used
     to finance the closure of the BKK Landfill Class I disposal area,
     Edison and other generators of hazardous waste disposed at the site,
     agreed to guarantee repayment of portions of the obligation in the
     event of default by BKK Corporation.

2.    The Edison portion was $1,280,000.00 and represents its maximum
     liability under the Agreement. No other liability was expressly or
     impliedly accepted by Edison or any of the other guarantors, although
     such disclaimers may not be affected by provisions of the state or
     federal law applicable to generators of hazardous waste.

3.    Payment of the bond indebtedness is scheduled to be completed on July
     15, 1996. BKK Corporation is current in its scheduled payments and at
     no time since the making of the Agreement has a default occurred.

4.    Among the affirmative obligations of BKK Corporation under the
     Agreement is the obligation to implement the Closure Plan and to obtain
     its Class III landfill permits. Edison and the other guarantors retain
     a right of specific performance.

Elizabeth L. Hanna, Esq.
December 17, 1990
Page 2


I have discussed your request with Bob Levy and wish to confirm that
this Company does not have an opinion on whether a conflict of interest does
or would exist if the Councilman participates in all or any particular
matter affecting the BKK Landfill.

Please call me if I can be of further assistance.

Very truly yours,

Ronald R. Gastelum
General Counsel




RRG1078:esg

sections.question: Whether Councilmember Herfert has a potential conflict of interest on matters concerning BKK because of Edison's position as a guarantor of BKK's obligation.
sections.analysis:
. 
My direct t j.ne telephone number is (714) 662-4644.
sections.question_synthetic: Does a City Councilmember employed by Southern California Edison have a conflict of interest in voting on matters affecting a landfill operator when Edison has guaranteed portions of the landfill operator's financial obligations?
sections.parsing_notes: Format: ocr; LLM: This is an incoming request to the FPPC, not an FPPC response. The document includes the initial request letter from the City Attorney and an attached letter from BKK Corporation's General Counsel providing factual background. No CONCLUSION or FPPC response is present in this document. OCR quality is moderate with some scanning artifacts visible.

embedding.summary: This is an incoming request letter from the City Attorney of West Covina seeking FPPC advice on whether Councilmember Steve Herfert, an Edison employee, has a conflict of interest regarding BKK landfill matters, given Edison's role as a guarantor of BKK's bond obligations. The letter includes background on the landfill operations and Edison's $1,280,000 maximum liability guarantee.
